input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's a complete `AppShell.xaml` setup:

**AppShell.xaml**
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="YourApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:YourApp.Pages"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
       Title="YourApp">

    <!-- Dashboard (single page) -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products (two tabs) -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**
```csharp
namespace YourApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register the route for programmatic navigation
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Pages.ProductDetailsPage),
                              typeof(Pages.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Navigating to the details page** (e.g. from `ActiveProductsPage`):

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

And receive the parameter via a query attribute:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(ProductId), "id")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public string ProductId { get; set; }
}
```

**Key points:**

- `FlyoutItem` creates a flyout menu entry. Nesting `Tab` elements inside one gives you the bottom tab bar for Products.
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` defers page creation until first navigation (lazy loading).
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` is needed for pages **not** declared in the visual hierarchy — it enables `GoToAsync` navigation to `ProductDetailsPage` as a pushed page on the current navigation stack.
